title: Company Dissolution Services in Vietnam
metaDescription: Read our complete guide to company dissolution in Vietnam and how WTP Advisory's services can simplify the process while ensuring compliance.

hero.title: Company Dissolution Services in Vietnam
hero.subtitle: If you choose to cease operations and close your company in Vietnam, you need to follow the necessary procedures for deregistering your company with the appropriate authorities. We are here to help you complete this process in a compliant and expedited manner.

sections.heading: Different Forms of Company Dissolution in Vietnam
body: Company Dissolution in Vietnam means ending a business officially. This process follows rules from the Law on Enterprises (LOE). There are two main ways it can happen:

sections.heading: Voluntary Dissolution
body: Voluntary dissolution is when owners decide to close a company, settling debts, and dividing assets among themselves for their company's best interest. Voluntary dissolution can occur in the following situations:

items: When the specified operating period in the company's charter expires, and the owners choose not to extend it further.
When the owners decide to discontinue business operations due to the recognition of the company's inefficiency.

sections.heading: Compulsory Dissolution
body: Compulsory dissolution is the termination of a business or company that is mandated or enforced by government authorities due to non-compliance with legal provisions or regulations. This occurs when:

items: A business violates established laws, leading to the mandatory cessation of all its business activities.
A business fails to meet the minimum requirements for members or shareholders as stipulated by regulatory agencies and is unable to rectify the deficiency within a specified period.

sections.heading: Requirements of Company Dissolution in Vietnam

items.title: Completion of Operation Term
items.desc: When the charter-specified operational duration ends without an extension decision.

items.title: Resolutions and Decisions
items.desc: Dissolution can occur through resolutions by business owners, Members' Council (for partnerships and LLCs), or Shareholders' Meeting (for joint-stock companies).

items.title: Insufficient Membership
items.desc: If the company doesn't meet the minimum member count for six consecutive months and doesn't transform its enterprise type as required by the law, dissolution becomes an option.

items.title: Revocation of Enterprise Registration
items.desc: If the enterprise registration certificate is revoked, dissolution follows unless the Law on Tax Administration specifies otherwise.

body: Note: Dissolution can only happen after settling all debts and property obligations. It's not allowed during ongoing dispute resolutions. In case of certificate revocation, both the manager and the enterprise share debt responsibility.

sections.heading: Company Dissolution Procedures in Vietnam
sections.subtitle: In general, to start implementing the process of dissolving as the resolutions and decisions on dissolutions of the company, the following steps must be taken:

items.title: Step 1
items.desc: Send the notice of dissolution of the enterprise to the Business Registration Office, with relevant documentation, within 7 days from the date of adoption of the resolution or decision on dissolution.

items.title: Step 2
items.desc: Within 1 working day from the date of receipt of the Notice, the Business Registration Office will post on the Portal, change the legal status of the enterprise to "In progress of dissolution procedures" and transfer information to Tax Authorities. Enterprises must then carry out the procedures for completing tax obligations to the Tax Authorities.

items.title: Step 3
items.desc: Enterprises liquidate debts in the following order: unpaid salaries, severance pay, social insurance, health insurance and benefits; tax debts; and other debts. Note: An enterprise may only be dissolved after all of its debts and liabilities are fully paid off and it is not involved in any dispute at local courts or arbitration.

items.title: Step 4
items.desc: Submit the application for business dissolution to the Business Registration Office. Note: Before proceeding, businesses must close related branches or offices at the local Business Registration Office, and return the seal and certificate to the police during dissolution if using their seal.

items.title: Step 5
items.desc: After receiving the Registration, they shall send information to the Tax Authorities, which shall reply within 2 working days to confirm fulfilment of the tax payment obligation. If the Business Registration Office does not receive a refusal from the Tax Authorities, they change the legal status of the enterprise to "Dissolved" and issue a notice of dissolution.

sections.heading: Legal Procedures of Company Dissolution
body: Our HCMC Company Dissolution Service handles all steps, from advice to dossier submission, ensuring a seamless and efficient dissolution for your company.

items: Consult your business on requirements
Collect your business's information, dossiers
Draft application forms for all steps
Submit the Notice on dissolution intent to HCMC Department of Planning and Investment
Return of Corporate seal if granted by HCMC Public Security Department
Submit complete application dossier for company dissolution to HCMC Department of Planning and Investment after completion of enterprise dissolution procedure at Tax Authority

sections.heading: Accounting Procedures of Company Dissolution
body: WTP Advisory's Accounting Procedures for Company Dissolution ensure a seamless process, from document review to tax reconciliation, providing a hassle-free conclusion. The Accounting Procedures in Company Dissolution Service helps:

items: Check and review all of the accounting supporting documents
Close accounting books and prepare the final financial statements
Support the independent audit for the final auditing
Prepare the Letter for Taxation Confirmation to confirm the tax obligation status of the Company, working with the Tax Authority to do the tax reconciliation
Prepare essential dissolution documents including liquidation notice, board minutes, company decision, and financial statements
Submit dissolution documentation to the Tax Authority and maintain regular communication to expedite their processes
Tax authorities perform tax audits, and we act as intermediaries, communicating and providing explanations to the authorities on your behalf
Work and communicate with the Tax authority for completion of tax obligations and lock tax code
Recover the stamp, ERC, and conclude the dissolution process

sections.heading: HR Procedures of Company Dissolution
body: HR Procedures in Company Dissolution Service from WTP Advisory simplifies the process by assisting with labor requirements, compensation agreements, data collection, financial calculations, and online reporting for a seamless dissolution. The HR Procedures in Company Dissolution Service helps:

items: Consult you on labor-related requirements
Meet with affected employees to get their mutual agreements on compensation
Draft mutual agreements on termination of labor contracts between you and affected employees
Gather all payroll data and employee data regarding their incurred PIT with your business
Calculate the total amount of compensation that you need to pay to affected employees
Calculate the pending PIT/SI amount that you need to pay
Produce quarterly and annual PIT reports for online submission
Issue confirmation letter of income and/or e-PIT certificate
